item.title: Microsoft’s Sliding Stock, AI Woes Make It Market’s Biggest Drag
item.description: The S&P 500 Index has had a fitful 2026 as it aims for a fourth-straight year of double-digit percentage gains, something it hasn’t done since the 1990s, while contending with risks from the war in Iran and rising inflation.

item.title: German Weapons Maker Rheinmetall Selling First Bond in 16 Years
item.description: Rheinmetall AG has begun selling €500 million ($580 million) of new debt in its first public bond offering since 2010.

item.title: Manus Weighs Raising $1 Billion to Unwind Meta Takeover
item.description: The co-founders of Manus are exploring options to fulfill Beijing’s demand to unwind a controversial takeover by Meta Platforms Inc., including raising about $1 billion from external investors to buy back the Chinese-founded AI operation.

item.title: UK Private Sector Shrinks as Starmer Crisis Adds to Iran Shock
item.description: British businesses posted the first decline in output in over a year as the Iran shock and a mounting rebellion against Prime Minister Keir Starmer hit activity in the services sector, a survey showed.

item.title: Morocco Backs Gaming Firms in Push to Spawn First ‘Unicorns’
item.description: Morocco will provide financial backing to local gaming startups, part of a state-sponsored push to generate more skilled jobs in the creative industries, diversify the economy and boost export revenue.

item.title: Samsung Chip Workers to Get Average $340,000 Bonus in AI Boom
item.description: Samsung Electronics Co. could distribute about 40 trillion won ($26.6 billion) to chip employees as a bonus for this year after the company struck a last-minute deal with labor unions to avert a strike.

item.title: China Warns EU of Retaliation as Bloc Mulls Tougher Trade Curbs
item.description: China will take countermeasures if the European Union presses ahead with new restrictions on Chinese imports, a Ministry of Commerce official said, as trade tensions between the two blocs continue to rise.

item.title: Iran Reviewing Trump’s Latest Offer as Clock Ticks on Ceasefire
item.description: Iran is assessing the latest peace proposal from the US, as the countries seek to avoid a resumption of hostilities.

item.title: Why Cerebras CEO Andrew Feldman Built The World’s Largest Computer Chip
item.description: Bigger means faster in the world of AI chipmaking.

item.title: Odd Lots: Why Cerebras Built The Largest Computer Chip (Podcast)
item.description: Size is the name of the game for the AI chipmaker Cerebras: Their chips are truly massive, about the size of a dinner plate. According to Andrew Feldman, CEO and founder of Cerebras, that is about 58 times larger than the average chip. That sheer size enables blazing fast inference for AI queries. Feldman joins us on the week of his company’s IPO to talk about his core product and how it fits into the AI boom. We discuss the history of the GPU, competition between open-and closed-source models,

item.title: Euro-Zone Business Activity Shrinks at Fastest Since 2023
item.description: Business activity in the euro area shrank at the quickest pace in 2 1/2 years, adding to fears that the Iran war and accompanying surge in energy costs are dealing a severe blow to the economy.

item.title: India Seeks Information from Capital Group in Front-Running Case
item.description: India’s market regulator has sought information from Capital Group about trades by its entities in the local stock market that were flagged in a front-running case in early 2025, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Taiwan Seeks to Detain Three in AI Chip Smuggling Crackdown
item.description: Taiwanese officials are seeking to detain three individuals for forging documents in order to export Nvidia Corp. AI chips to China, the island democracy’s first such crackdown on semiconductor smuggling.

item.title: Starling’s Profit Dips as UK Fintech Looks to Move Past Restrictions
item.description: UK fintech Starling Group’s revenue dropped almost 6% in the year through March as it worked to offboard certain customers to come into regulatory compliance and invested more across its business.

item.title: German Private-Sector Activity Contracts for Second Month on War
item.description: German private-sector activity shrank for a second month, raising the risk that Europe’s largest economy is succumbing to the knock-on effects of the Iran war.

item.title: Saudi Oil Exports Value Surged to Three-Year High in March
item.description: Saudi Arabia’s revenue from oil exports jumped to a more than three-year high of $24.7 billion in the first full month of the war in the Middle East, as the kingdom’s ability to divert shipments through the Red Sea coupled with higher energy prices compensated for disruptions from the closure of the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: French Business Activity Slumps at Fastest Pace Since 2020
item.description: French business activity shrank at the quickest pace in five and a half years as higher energy prices hit consumers and firms.

item.title: Sri Lankan Rupee Weakens to Three-Year Low as Oil Gains Weigh
item.description: The Sri Lankan rupee weakened to a three-year low on Thursday, underperforming all Asian peers, as a gain in oil prices weighed on the currency.

item.title: South Africa Gains Lift Investec Profit, Dividend to Record
item.description: Investec posted a record dividend for the fourth straight year as the South Africa- and UK-listed bank’s full-year profit climbed to a new high.

item.title: BT Sees Revenue Declines as UK Competition Rises
item.description: BT Group Plc forecast declining revenues for 2027 that missed analyst estimates, as Chief Executive Officer Allison Kirkby continues a turnaround plan to stem customer losses and fend off growing competition.

item.title: AMD Commits More Than $10 Billion to Taiwan AI Investments
item.description: Advanced Micro Devices Inc., the leading challenger to Nvidia Corp. in AI computing chips, pledged to invest more than $10 billion in Taiwan to expand partnerships and add packaging capacity.

item.title: South Korea Set to Reduce Debt Sales in June as Bonds Slump
item.description: South Korea will cut its debt issuance in June, the finance ministry said, as the government steps up its defense of the bond market.

item.title: Stock Rally Takes a Breather on Rebound in Oil Prices
UK and Gulf States Hail Trade Deal After Four Years of Talks
item.description: The UK signed a long-awaited trade deal with the Gulf Cooperation Council on Thursday, deepening its ties with the oil-rich nations at a time of rising geopolitical tension and economic pressures.

item.title: India’s Economic Activity Holds Steady in May, Flash PMIs Show
item.description: India’s economic activity remained stable in May from a month ago, a flash survey by HSBC Holdings Plc showed Thursday.

item.title: Circular AI Boom Goes Global as Asia Windfall Funds Hyperscalers
item.description: The windfall earned by Asian chip makers is coursing through the world economy, mirroring on a global scale the circular flow of money within the AI ecosystem.

item.title: Norway’s Europe-Leading Stock Rally Goes Beyond Oil Heavyweights
item.description: Norway is developed Europe’s best-performing stock market this year, thanks to surging energy prices. But there’s more attracting investors to Oslo than just oil and gas.

item.title: Oil Near $100 Emerges as Consensus for Next Year With Iran War
item.description: Oil market participants are increasingly pricing crude to be capped near $100 a barrel over the next year, as demand is forced to slow down to counter millions of barrels of supply losses caused by the US-Iran war.

item.title: Nvidia’s Huang Spurs Asia Tech Rally With AI, Robots Hype
item.description: The latest report from Nvidia Corp. may have left its investors wanting more but it gave a jolt to the shares of a broad swath of Asian tech companies.

item.title: Seoul Apartment Price Rally Gains Pace Ahead of BOK Decision
item.description: The housing rally in South Korea’s capital continues to regain momentum, an outcome that is likely to reinforce concerns at the nation’s central bank that rising property prices could complicate the policy outlook as the Iran conflict fuels inflationary pressures.
